blocks.content: She picked up her brush again but didn't paint.

He was still sitting on the edge of the paint table. The distance between them was about two feet — not far, not near, the particular middle distance that had become something charged without either of them deciding it.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: What are you drawing in your head?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You. The way you look right now.
She turned fully to face him.
She was still holding the brush. There was a small smudge of blue on her left index finger from when she'd touched the wet canvas.

He had been painting most of his life. He understood negative space — the shape defined by what isn't there, the boundary between two things. He was, right now, acutely aware of the negative space between them.
She took one step. Not quite all the way.
He didn't move. He understood, in a way that was more than decision, that the right thing here was to let her bring herself to him. That some things had to be chosen, not caught.
She looked at him. He looked at her.
